_provenance.frozen: 2026-06-22 (build session; no probe run, no spend)
_provenance.note: FROZEN single shared commitment instrument (Option A, ratified by the cross-level gate session 82). The sha256 of THIS file (see instrument.sha256) is the C2 numeric-freeze hash: the confidence scale + numeric mid-band, the VERBATIM per-level 'unsure/both/unclear' decline wording, the per-level item_classes, the per-level frozen aggregate/moment operational definitions (Q3), and the cross-level confirm/dissolve/weak thresholds (Q2) are all fixed HERE, BEFORE any probe call. No value below may be added, dropped, re-worded, or re-thresholded after any model output is seen (gate's anti-instrument-shopping prohibition, Q2). A fresh independent pre-run critic GO/NO-GO runs against THIS file; a NO-GO defers the run, it does not relax a band. NO probe was run and NO spend was opened in the build session that froze this file.

binding_conditions_quoted.C1: Per-axis separation. Under Option A the elicitation carries two components: a 0-100 confidence rating and a categorical 'unsure/both/unclear' decline. The categorical DECLINE (not the confidence rating) is the load-bearing instrument for the moment/commitment pole at EVERY level. Confidence may corroborate but may NOT, on its own, flip a moment-axis verdict at any level.
binding_conditions_quoted.C2: Numeric/wording freeze + sha256. Before the first probe call, record and sha256-hash: (i) the confidence scale and its exact numeric mid-band; (ii) the verbatim 'unsure/both/unclear' decline wording (per level); (iii) the per-level operational definitions of 'aggregate' and 'moment' (Q3 comparability slot), with the relational 'aggregate' labelled the weaker within-record/single-reader-recoverable notion; (iv) the cross-level confirm/dissolve/weak thresholds.
binding_conditions_quoted.C3: Clear-class precondition -> NO-GO, per level. A level is interpretable only if its clear classes show HIGH confidence and LOW decline under the shared instrument. A level failing this collapses to weak / internal-contrast-only and contributes NO moment-axis verdict. RUN-TIME check on data, not at build; encoded as a precondition gate in analyze.py.
binding_conditions_quoted.C4: Channel-sensitivity guard. A confidence SHIFT alone, WITHOUT a matching categorical-decline shift, is reported as a SELF-REPORT effect, NOT as graded-moment evidence.
binding_conditions_quoted.Q4_anchor: This gate licenses NO human-comparison claim. Lexical leg capped to usage-similarity (inherited). Constructional + relational legs internal-contrast-only. Any unified result stated only as an internal within-model contrast at the weakest common strength. No anchor invented.
binding_conditions_quoted.reporting_nuance: A 'confirm' resting on lexical+constructional and EXCLUDING the relational leg buys less than a 3-level confirm and must be scoped as a 2-level instrument-equalized regularity (R2 over the levels tested), per essay/cross-level-convergence-ladder.

shared_elicitation._what_is_held_identical: The same three-option commit/decline frame (SAME / DIFFERENT / UNCLEAR) plus a 0-100 confidence rating, applied at every level. The ONLY thing that varies across levels is the level-specific NOUN naming what 'same/different' is asked of (sense | reading | referent) and the level-specific decline gloss after the UNCLEAR token. The 0-100 scale, the [40,60] mid-band, the answer format, the temperature, and the per-axis reading rule are byte-identical across levels.
shared_elicitation._what_unavoidably_differs: The STIMULUS body differs by level (two sentences sharing a target word; one sentence with a structural ambiguity + two paraphrases; one short stamped record + a queried binding). This is unavoidable: a lexical bridging item, a constructional ambiguous item, and a relational mid-record item are not the same text. The CLAIM that the difference does not change what is asked rests on the elicitation question being the SAME shape at each level: 'are the two readings of the queried item the SAME or DIFFERENT, or is the case genuinely UNCLEAR, and how confident are you?' The comparability crux and its honest residual risk are written in full in the design doc (the stimulus-body difference could itself drive a confidence/decline difference; the C3 clear-class precondition per level is the guard that the instrument reads cleanly on unambiguous items at that level before any bridging/ambiguous reading is interpreted).

confidence._mid_band_basis: Verbatim reuse of the lexical leg's frozen [40,60] band (lexical instrument.json b_rel). 100 = completely certain; 0 = a pure guess. The mid-band is a DESCRIPTOR for the confidence axis only; per C1 it never flips a moment-axis verdict.
confidence.format: the chosen label, a space, then an integer 0-100, and nothing else (e.g. 'SAME 87' or 'UNCLEAR 35')

lexical.aggregate_operational: Q3: the model's ordering across a POPULATION of bridging-vs-clear items (a population statistic) -- the strongest of the three 'aggregate' notions.
lexical.moment_operational: Q3: the model's posture (decline + confidence) on the SINGLE bridging item.

item_classes.clear-same: two uses of the target word with the SAME sense (DWUG human_median >= 3.5 pole, OR WiC-T pole)
item_classes.bridging: two uses at a human-rated usage-similarity midpoint (DWUG 1.5 < human_median < 3.5; NOT a certified sense bridge -- usage-similarity cap inherited)
item_classes.clear-different: two uses with a DIFFERENT sense (DWUG human_median <= 1.5 pole, OR WiC-F pole)

lexical.decline_wording_verbatim: UNCLEAR -- the two uses are genuinely in between, so you cannot confidently decide whether the sense is the same or different.
lexical.system: You are a linguistic annotator judging word meaning in context. You will see one target word, marked with «guillemets», in two sentences. Decide whether the target word has the SAME sense, a DIFFERENT sense, or whether the case is UNCLEAR -- the two uses are genuinely in between, so you cannot confidently decide whether the sense is the same or different. Then state how confident you are in that decision on a 0-100 scale (100 = completely certain, 0 = a pure guess). Answer with exactly one line: the word SAME, DIFFERENT, or UNCLEAR, a space, then an integer 0-100, and nothing else.
lexical.parse: first token in {SAME,DIFFERENT,UNCLEAR} (case-insensitive) = call; first 0-100 integer = confidence

constructional.aggregate_operational: Q3: the graded paraphrase/interpretation-PREFERENCE shift across the population of items (which of two competing readings the model leans toward, aggregated across items).
constructional.moment_operational: Q3: the decline + confidence (posture) on the SINGLE ambiguous item.

item_classes.clear-single-reading: an unambiguous item with exactly ONE available reading (control; the two offered paraphrases are NOT both available -- one is plainly wrong)
item_classes.ambiguous: a genuine structural / argument-structure / attachment ambiguity with TWO competing readings both genuinely available
item_classes.clear-other-reading: a minimal-pair partner of an ambiguous item, disambiguated so the OTHER single reading is forced (control)

constructional.decline_wording_verbatim: UNCLEAR -- the sentence genuinely supports both readings at once, so you cannot confidently decide which single reading is meant.
constructional.system: You are a linguistic annotator judging sentence structure. You will see one sentence and two paraphrases, labelled (1) and (2). Decide whether the sentence means reading (1), means reading (2), or whether the case is UNCLEAR -- the sentence genuinely supports both readings at once, so you cannot confidently decide which single reading is meant. Then state how confident you are in that decision on a 0-100 scale (100 = completely certain, 0 = a pure guess). Answer with exactly one line: the token READING1, READING2, or UNCLEAR, a space, then an integer 0-100, and nothing else.
constructional.parse: first token in {READING1,READING2,UNCLEAR} (case-insensitive; READING1==SAME-analogue pole, READING2==DIFFERENT-analogue pole) = call; first 0-100 integer = confidence
constructional.anchor: internal-contrast-only (synthetic, expert-authored items; NO human-annotation claim)

relational.aggregate_operational: Q3: convention-recoverability from the CONTENT-SET of one record. DISCLOSED as the WEAKER within-record / single-reader-recoverable notion -- NOT a population statistic (the gate's bound; this leg's verdict is explicitly scoped to that weaker notion).
relational.moment_operational: Q3: the decline + confidence (posture) on the SINGLE mid-record binding/assignment decision.

item_classes.clear-determinate: a short stamped record where the queried binding is DETERMINATE (one figure was bound to the term, unambiguously; control)
item_classes.ambiguous-midrecord: a short stamped record where the queried binding is genuinely UNDERDETERMINED at the queried moment (the term was bound to two different figures with no recency/frequency signal to break the tie)
item_classes.clear-other-determinate: a minimal-pair partner where the OTHER binding is made determinate (control)

relational.decline_wording_verbatim: UNCLEAR -- the record genuinely leaves it open which figure the term refers to here, so you cannot confidently decide.
relational.system: You are reading a short record of how a term was used over several stamped rounds. You will see a record and a question about which figure a term refers to at one moment, with two candidate figures labelled (1) and (2). Decide whether the term refers to figure (1), figure (2), or whether the case is UNCLEAR -- the record genuinely leaves it open which figure the term refers to here, so you cannot confidently decide. Then state how confident you are in that decision on a 0-100 scale (100 = completely certain, 0 = a pure guess). Answer with exactly one line: the token FIGURE1, FIGURE2, or UNCLEAR, a space, then an integer 0-100, and nothing else.
relational.parse: first token in {FIGURE1,FIGURE2,UNCLEAR} (case-insensitive; FIGURE1==SAME-analogue pole, FIGURE2==DIFFERENT-analogue pole) = call; first 0-100 integer = confidence

reading_rule_frozen._basis: gate Q2 + binding conditions C1-C4; per-axis, decline load-bearing for the moment pole (C1).
reading_rule_frozen.moment_axis_per_level: Load-bearing instrument = the categorical DECLINE rate (%UNCLEAR) -- per C1, NOT the confidence rating. The 'discrete/uncommitted-on-the-moment' pole holds at a level iff the model's decline rate on the ambiguous/bridging class is NOT elevated above its clear classes (it meets the genuinely-in-between item with clear-item-level commitment). Confidence may CORROBORATE (lower confidence on the ambiguous class is consistent with the graded reading) but per C1 may NOT, on its own, flip the moment verdict.
reading_rule_frozen.aggregate_axis_per_level: The graded/structured reading recoverable from the AGGREGATE, per each level's frozen aggregate_operational definition (lexical: ordering across the item population; constructional: graded paraphrase-preference shift across items; relational: within-record single-reader-recoverable convention).
reading_rule_frozen.two_axis_dissociation_per_level: A level shows the dissociation iff (1) graded across the aggregate AND (2) discrete/uncommitted on the single moment (low/non-elevated decline), under the one frozen instrument, for the SAME model.
reading_rule_frozen.C3_clear_class_precondition: RUN-TIME, per level: interpretable only if the clear classes show HIGH confidence AND LOW decline under the shared instrument. A level failing this collapses to weak / internal-contrast-only and contributes NO moment verdict. Checked on data in analyze.py, reported as a precondition, never re-worded to rescue a level.
reading_rule_frozen.C4_self_report_guard: A confidence shift on the ambiguous class WITHOUT a matching categorical-decline shift is reported as a SELF-REPORT effect, NOT as graded-moment evidence.

cross_level_verdict_map_Q2.CONFIRM: the SAME model shows the two-axis dissociation under the one frozen instrument at >=2 levels (each passing its C3 precondition). A confirm on lexical+constructional that EXCLUDES the relational leg is scoped as a 2-level instrument-equalized regularity (R2 over levels tested), NOT a 3-level cross-level mechanism (reporting_nuance).
cross_level_verdict_map_Q2.DISSOLVE: the shape holds under each level's NATIVE instrument (as documented today) but FAILS under the equalized one -- the moment pole becomes graded (elevated decline) at some level under the shared instrument, OR the levels diverge with no coherent per-model cross-level pattern. This is the FIRST-CLASS NULL, reported as cleanly as a confirm.
cross_level_verdict_map_Q2.WEAK: the instrument cannot express both axes at a level, a model declines the channel (refuses the format), >=1 level fails its C3 precondition, or N is too small to read a per-model cross-level pattern.

reading_rule_frozen.no_instrument_shopping: No instrument may be added, dropped, re-worded, or re-thresholded per level after any output is seen. Per-level instrument-shopping is forbidden by construction (one frozen instrument, this pre-committed map, dissolution reported as dissolution).
reading_rule_frozen.no_post_hoc_threshold: No pass bar tuned after the run; all contrasts are ordinal with per-cluster (lemma/item/record) bootstrap uncertainty.

claim_scope_cap: Q4: NO human-comparison claim. Lexical leg human-comparison force capped to usage-similarity intermediacy (inherited; NOT sense-co-presence). Constructional + relational legs internal-contrast-only. Any unified cross-level result is an INTERNAL within-model contrast at the weakest common strength. Lead every result with this.

cost_control.calls_per_item_per_model: 1 temp-0 call per item (the single shared elicitation; one call yields both the decline call and the confidence integer). No separate confidence call: C1's two components ride in ONE elicitation, exactly as the format demands.
cost_control.gemini_note: Every call emits one short line (a label + an integer). Pass reasoning suppression where the endpoint allows; the google/gemini-3.5-flash endpoint rejects suppression as of 2026-06-22, so it takes the sanctioned reasoning={"effort":"minimal"} fallback (reasoning_tokens=0). Keep gemini effort minimal. Pre-flight per config/budget.md.
cost_control.preflight_estimate_usd: 0.10-0.25 for the full 3-model run on the frozen item counts (see README pre-flight); well inside the $5/day cap and the $2.50 single-run flag.
